SCROLL
Why now

Türkiye doesn't currently tax crypto gains — not because of a generous law, but because no specific law exists yet.

Here's the honest picture, plainly stated. We'd rather you understand exactly where things stand than sell you a promise we can't back.

Today

A regulatory gap, not a guarantee

Crypto gains currently fall outside Türkiye's existing income tax framework. There is no crypto-specific tax law on the books — this is an absence of regulation, not a deliberate 0% policy.

In progress

A 10% withholding tax bill is in parliament

Legislation has been introduced proposing a 10% withholding tax on crypto gains from regulated platforms, with the president able to adjust the rate anywhere from 0% to 20% depending on token type and holding period.

Also proposed

A 20-year foreign-income exemption

A separate, larger proposal would exempt qualifying new residents from Turkish tax on foreign-source income for up to 20 years. This is the real "0%" story — broader than crypto, and still awaiting full parliamentary approval.

Bottom line

Move for the country. Let the tax upside be a bonus.

We won't tell you this is locked in forever, because it isn't. What we will tell you is that the window is open now, residency takes real time to establish, and the people who move early are positioned well whichever way the law lands.

Regulatory status last reviewed: July 2026 — laws referenced are proposed and subject to change.

Life in Türkiye

The tax case is the hook. The country is the reason people stay.

Cost of living, without the compromise

Day-to-day costs sit meaningfully below Western Europe, the UK, and the US — without giving up quality housing or a genuinely global city.

Istanbul is not a backup city

Direct flights to nearly everywhere, a real culinary and cultural scene, and a financial center actively investing in itself.

Coast or city — Türkiye offers both

Bodrum and Antalya for a slower, coastal pace; Istanbul for density and opportunity. Most clients use both.

Property as an asset, not just a visa cost

The real estate that qualifies you for residency is also a real, appreciating asset — not a sunk cost.

How it works

A clear sequence, start to finish.

01

Consultation

We assess your situation, your goals, and which pathway actually fits — before you commit to anything.

02

Pathway selection & documentation

We confirm the route and prepare the required documentation together.

03

Property or residency application

We coordinate the property search where relevant, and submit and track your application.

04

Banking, tax registration & counsel referral

We set up local banking and connect you with our licensed Turkish tax and legal partners.

05

Life setup

Housing, schools if relevant, local integration — the parts that turn a legal status into an actual life.

Questions

Straight answers to the questions that actually matter.

Right now, crypto gains fall outside existing Turkish tax law — but that's a regulatory gap, not a permanent guarantee. A bill proposing a 10% crypto withholding tax is already in parliament. A separate, larger 20-year foreign-income exemption has also been proposed but isn't law yet.

Turkish tax residency and the underlying benefits of living there don't disappear if the crypto-specific rules change. Our ongoing advisory exists to help you adjust as regulations evolve.

Property-based residency can often be established within months. Citizenship by investment generally takes around three years. Exact timelines depend on your case.

No — Türkiye permits dual citizenship. Whether your home country allows dual citizenship is worth checking on your end.

No. We coordinate your relocation and connect you with licensed Turkish tax and legal professionals for anything regulated.

Yes. Details shared during consultation are handled discreetly and only shared with licensed partners where necessary.

Didn't find your question? Use the chat launcher in the corner — it's built to answer relocation questions in real time.

Muro Türkiye provides relocation guidance and coordination only. We are not a licensed tax advisor or law firm; regulated tax and legal work is referred to independent licensed Turkish partners. Regulatory information on this site reflects proposed and current law as of July 2026 and is subject to change — confirm final details with licensed counsel before making financial decisions.
